reference: GATE CS — Computer Networks / Network Layer

formulas.name: Hosts per Subnet
formulas.plain: Usable hosts in a /p subnet = 2^(32-p) - 2 (subtract network and broadcast)
formulas.whenToUse: Counting assignable host addresses in a subnet.
formulas.trap: Subtract 2 for network + broadcast addresses (point-to-point /31 is the exception).

formulas.name: Block / Subnet Size
formulas.plain: Addresses per block = 2^(32-p); network address is the first, broadcast the last
formulas.trap: Network address = (IP AND mask); broadcast = network OR (NOT mask).

formulas.name: Number of Subnets
formulas.plain: Borrowing k host bits gives 2^k subnets
formulas.trap: Classic 'subnet-zero' debates aside, modern CIDR allows all 2^k subnets.

formulas.name: Route Aggregation (Supernetting)
formulas.plain: Aggregate N contiguous equal blocks by shortening the prefix by log2(N) bits
formulas.whenToUse: Summarising routes into one CIDR entry.
formulas.trap: Blocks must be contiguous and aligned on the aggregate boundary.

formulas.name: Fragment Offset
formulas.plain: Fragment offset field counts in units of 8 bytes = (data bytes before)/8
formulas.whenToUse: Computing offset fields when an IP datagram is fragmented.
formulas.trap: Every fragment except the last must carry a data length that is a multiple of 8 bytes.

formulas.name: Max Data per Fragment
formulas.plain: Max payload per fragment = floor((MTU - header)/8) * 8 (multiple of 8)
formulas.whenToUse: Splitting a datagram across a link with a given MTU.
formulas.trap: Round the data DOWN to a multiple of 8; only the last fragment may be non-multiple.

formulas.name: IPv4 Header Size
formulas.plain: IPv4 header is 20 bytes without options, up to 60 with options; total-length field in bytes
formulas.trap: Total Length counts header + data in bytes; IHL counts header in 4-byte words.

formulas.name: TTL Decrement
formulas.plain: Each router decrements TTL by 1; at 0 the packet is dropped and ICMP Time Exceeded is sent
formulas.trap: TTL counts hops (routers), not seconds, in practice.
